dataset_overview.title: Dataset Overview & Target Variable

explanation.what: Initial scan of the dataset to understand its shape, feature types, and the distribution of the target variable (streams).
explanation.why: Crucial for identifying the scale of the problem and ensuring the target variable is clean and numeric.
explanation.how: Using pandas for shape and dtypes, and Plotly for visualizing the stream distribution.

dataset_overview.plotly:
import plotly.express as px
fig = px.histogram(df, x='streams', title='Distribution of Streams')
fig.show()
dataset_overview.matplotlib:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.hist(df['streams'])
plt.title('Distribution of Streams')
plt.show()
dataset_overview.seaborn:
import seaborn as sns
sns.histplot(df['streams'])
plt.title('Distribution of Streams')
plt.show()

missing_values.title: Missing Value Analysis

explanation.what: Identifying null or NaN values across all features.
explanation.why: Missing data can bias models and cause errors in statistical calculations.
explanation.how: Using df.isnull().sum() and visualizing with a bar chart.

missing_values.plotly:
import plotly.graph_objects as go
missing = df.isnull().sum()
fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Bar(x=missing.index, y=missing.values)])
fig.show()

descriptive_stats.title: Descriptive Statistics

explanation.what: Calculating mean, median, standard deviation, and quartiles for numerical features.
explanation.why: Provides a mathematical summary of the data's central tendency and spread.

descriptive_stats.plotly:
# Plotly usually visualizes distributions rather than tables
fig = px.box(df, y='bpm')
fig.show()

explanation.what: Visualizing the frequency distribution of key features like Danceability and Energy.
explanation.why: Helps identify skewness, modality, and potential outliers.

explanation.what: Measuring the linear relationship between pairs of features.
explanation.why: Identifies which features are most strongly related to the target variable (streams).

explanation.what: Analyzing the contribution of individual artists to total stream counts.
explanation.why: Reveals market concentration and the 'Superstar Effect'.
explanation.how: Groupby artist and sum streams, then visualize with bar charts.

temporal_trends.title: Temporal Trends & Seasonality

explanation.what: Tracking how audio features and release volumes change over years and months.
explanation.why: Identifies industry shifts and optimal release windows.
explanation.how: Line charts for yearly averages and bar charts for monthly counts.

temporal_trends.plotly:
yearly = df.groupby('released_year').mean()
fig = px.line(yearly, y='danceability_%')
fig.show()

explanation.what: Comparing 'Mega Hits' vs 'Normal Hits' across various metrics.
explanation.why: Isolates the specific characteristics that lead to extreme success.

audio_profiles.title: Audio Profiles & Sonic Mood

explanation.what: Mapping the 'mood' of tracks using Energy and Valence.
explanation.why: Visualizes the emotional landscape of the dataset.
explanation.how: 2D scatter plots (Mood Maps) and Radar charts.

audio_profiles.plotly:
fig = px.scatter(df, x='valence_%', y='energy_%', color='mode')
fig.show()
